summary: Frontend-only mobile tab regression testing completed on public URL. Readability, horizontal scroll, and tab navigation passed at 390px and 360px. One stability regression remains: active tab still reports glowPulse animation in runtime styles.

ui_bugs.issue: Active tab still has runtime animationName='glowPulse' (3s) on mobile, which contradicts the intended no-animation stability change.

action_items: Ensure deployed CSS fully removes active-tab animation on mobile/runtime (verify final bundle and cache invalidation).
After fix, re-run mobile checks at 390px and 360px to confirm computed style animationName is 'none' for a.nav-link.active.
critical_code_review_comments: index.css is very large (4719 lines), making responsive/override debugging fragile; split nav/mobile rules into focused CSS modules.
Multiple nav-link.active style blocks exist across the stylesheet, increasing override ambiguity and deployment mismatch risk.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL tested: https://wc2026-nine-sepia.vercel.app. Verified 390px + 360px: nav labels visible (not icon-only), horizontal x-scroll works (no y drift), and tab routing works (Schedule/Groups/Top Scorers/Records). Runtime computed style still reports glowPulse on active tab, so animation-disable change may not be fully reflected in deployed bundle.
rca of the issue: Repro: open app on 390px, scroll nav, click 'Records', inspect computed style for a.nav-link.active => animationName: glowPulse, animationDuration: 3s. Possible cause: stale deployed CSS chunk/cache or conflicting later CSS rule in final artifact. Mitigation: verify deployed build hash includes latest nav CSS, clear/rotate service worker cache strategy, and consolidate active-tab rule to a single source of truth. MOCKED APIs: NONE.
